title: Your first edit

spec.title: Your first edit
spec.notes_for_generator: Give AI something you've written, ask it to improve it. Teach critique prompting — asking AI to explain what it changed and why, not just produce an edited version. The learner should bring their own real piece of writing. Guided: show one example of how to frame the critique request.

content:
SUBJECT: AI Gym Day 5: Make AI edit your work (not just rewrite it)

Most LLM-edited writing looks fine and feels like nothing. That's because "improve this" doesn't actually tell the AI what you want — it just tells it to do something.

TODAY'S WORKOUT
───────────────
✂️ EDITING | 🟢 WARM-UP | ⏱ 10–15 min

THE BRIEF:
You've got a piece of writing — an email, a summary, a bio, a slide note, anything you've drafted recently. Instead of asking AI to "make it better," you're going to ask it to edit with intention and then explain its choices. The goal isn't just a cleaner draft. It's understanding *what changed and why*, so you can use that thinking next time.

YOUR REPS:

1. Paste your writing into your chosen LLM and ask it to improve it for a specific purpose. Before you send the prompt, decide: what's the one thing this piece most needs? Shorter? Sharper opening? More confidence in tone? Tell it that, along with who'll read it and what you want them to do or feel. Don't just say "make it better."

2. Here's the technique you're adding today — ask the AI to show its work. After it gives you the edit, send this follow-up (adapted in your own words): *"Can you tell me the three most significant changes you made and why you made each one?"* — Then read its explanation. Did it identify the same weaknesses you suspected? Did it catch something you missed? Note any disagreement.

3. Now push back on one of its changes. Find an edit you're not sure about — something that feels off, or that you'd have done differently — and tell the AI why you're uncertain. Ask it to either defend the choice or offer an alternative. See how it handles the challenge.

SUCCESS CRITERIA:
Your output is good if:
• The edited version is doing a specific job better than the original — not just shorter or tidier, but more effective for the reader and purpose you named
• The model's explanation of its changes is specific enough that you could apply the same logic to a different piece of writing
• You've had at least one genuine back-and-forth — not just accepted the first output

💡 COACHING NOTE:
Asking an LLM to explain its edits turns a passive transaction into a critique. When you just ask for an improved draft, you get a result but no reasoning — and you can't learn from reasoning you never see. The same principle applies any time you're using an LLM to evaluate or transform something: ask for the *why* alongside the *what*, and the output becomes twice as useful. This works for feedback on presentations, reviews of plans, assessments of arguments — anywhere you'd otherwise be left guessing at the logic behind a suggestion.

──────────
Use only material you are allowed to share with your chosen LLM. Anonymise anything sensitive.
Share the programme: https://harrysharman.com/projects/ai-habit/
Found this useful? You can leave a tip: https://ko-fi.com/theaihabit
review_notes: Clarified that AI Habit focuses on chat-based large language models (LLMs), while using AI as public shorthand.
Kept exercises model-agnostic and usable with any text-based LLM or approved work tool.
Reworked research/reasoning language to avoid over-trusting model confidence or hidden chain-of-thought.
Reframed AI Habit as a grounded free 14-day starter block with a paid continuation for deeper 60-90 day habit-building, rather than a validation experiment.

Positioned the paid continuation as The 90-Day AI Habit: a structured applied-LLM programme covering writing/editing, strategy/decision-making, research/assessment, management/delegation, personal productivity, and practical outputs.
Simplified the 90-day commercial route: join individually, bring a small group for a discounted group subscription, or use the workplace launch pack to get an employer to pay.
Added ethical upgrade nudges to Days 3, 7, and 10: endowed progress, career identity, and momentum protection for The 90-Day AI Habit.
Mechanical repair pass: normalised workout badge line after scheduler verification.
